name_en: Ministry of Housing and Urban Development
description: Iran's Ministry of Housing and Urban Development oversaw housing policy, urban planning, and construction regulation. Under [Mohammad Saeedikia](/people/mohammad-saeedikia) (2005-2009), the ministry launched the Mehr Housing project, a massive subsidized construction program targeting low- and middle-income households across over 1,100 cities. The ministry was merged with the Ministry of Roads in 2011 to form the Ministry of Roads and Urban Development.
editorial_notes: Merged with Ministry of Roads in 2011. Mehr Housing project estimated to cost ~$150 billion.
